result.summary: PlanetScale exposes a REST API with an OpenAPI spec, two hosted MCP server endpoints, and the `pscale` CLI.
result.description: PlanetScale is a managed relational database platform for MySQL/Vitess and Postgres. It provides infrastructure and management features for databases, branches, schema workflows, and insights.

ps_service_token.label: PlanetScale service token ID + service token secret
ps_service_token.setup: In the PlanetScale dashboard, open [Settings → Service tokens](https://app.planetscale.com/~/settings/service-tokens), click **New service token**, copy both the token `ID` and token value, then assign the needed organization/database permissions. PlanetScale shows the secret only once.

ps_oauth_app.label: PlanetScale OAuth application (client ID + client secret)
ps_oauth_app.setup: In your PlanetScale organization, open [Settings → OAuth applications](https://app.planetscale.com/~/settings/oauth-applications), click **Create new application**, enter the app name, domain, and redirect URI, then save and copy the app `ID`, `client ID`, and `client secret` (shown once). Configure the scopes your app needs. Users then authorize the app to obtain OAuth access tokens for the API or hosted MCP server.

ps_cli_login.label: PlanetScale CLI login session
ps_cli_login.setup: Install the CLI, then run `pscale auth login`. PlanetScale opens a browser, asks you to sign in, and confirms the device code shown in the terminal. The resulting CLI auth token is managed by `pscale` and is valid for one month according to the CLI docs.
